text: The LORD told Noah to enter the ark with his family, for he alone was righteous in this generation.
Noah was to take seven pairs of every clean animal, and a pair of every unclean animal.
Also seven pairs of every bird, to keep every kind alive on the earth.
In seven days the LORD would send rain for 40 days and nights, wiping out every living thing he had made.
Noah did all the LORD commanded him.
Noah was 600 years old when the floodwaters came on the earth.
Noah and his wife, sons, and their wives entered the ark to escape the flood.
Clean and unclean animals, birds, and creeping things,
came to Noah in pairs, male and female, as God had commanded.
After seven days the floodwaters came on the earth.
In Noah's 600th year, on the 17th day of the second month, the springs of the deep burst open and the sky's floodgates opened.
Rain fell on the earth for 40 days and 40 nights.
That same day Noah and his family entered the ark.
With them went every kind of wild animal, livestock, creeping thing, and bird.
Pairs of every creature with the breath of life came to Noah in the ark.
Male and female of every kind entered, just as God commanded, and the LORD shut them in.
The flood continued 40 days, the waters increased and lifted the ark high above the earth.
The waters rose greatly and the ark floated on the surface.
The waters rose so high they covered all the high mountains under the whole sky.
The waters rose over 20 feet above the mountaintops.
Every living creature that moved on the earth perished — birds, livestock, wild animals, and people.
Everything on dry land with the breath of life in its nostrils died.
Every living thing on the face of the earth was wiped out; only Noah and those with him in the ark survived.
The floodwaters covered the earth for 150 days.
